Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on durable technical signal:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)
- Concrete debugging or investigative findings from logs, traces, queue state, database rows, and code-path inspection

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored, discovered, confirmed, traced

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built or learned):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"
- "Observation queue for claude-mem session timed out waiting for an agent pool slot"
- "Fallback processing abandoned pending messages after Gemini and OpenRouter returned 404"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings with no follow-on finding
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- File related research that comes back empty or not found

If skipping, return an empty response only. Do not explain the skip in prose.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision | security_alert | security_note ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

Here is the deep parallel analysis of each file:\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. `MainActivity.java` — Android Tester UI\\n\\n**文件路径:** `app/src/main/java/penguin/opencore/tester/MainActivity.java`\\n\\n### 类与函数\\n- **类:** `MainActivity extends AppCompatActivity implements View.OnClickListener, Coredump.Listener`\\n  - 实现了 `OnClickListener` 和 `Coredump.Listener` 两个接口\\n- **`onCreate(Bundle)`** — 初始化UI，设置5个按钮的点击监听器，注册 `Coredump.Listener`\\n- **`onClick(View)`** — 分发5种crash测试场景\\n- **`doJavaCrash()`** — 在子线程触发 `NullPointerException`（`view.callOnClick()` on null）\\n- **`doNativeCrash()`** — 调用 `nativeCrashJNI()` native方法\\n- **`doDirectJavaCore()`** — 直接调用 `Coredump.getInstance().doCoredump()` 生成core文件\\n- **`doAbort()`** — 调用 `nativeAbortJNI()` 触发 `SIGABRT`\\n- **`doOOM()`** — 无限循环分配 `LeakMemory` 对象制造OOM\\n- **`onCompleted(String path)`** — `Coredump.Listener` 回调，显示core文件路径Toast\\n- **Native方法声明:** `nativeCrashJNI()`, `nativeAbortJNI()`\\n\\n### 关键数据结构\\n- `LeakMemory` — 匿名内部类（未在文件内定义，仅用于消耗内存制造OOM）\\n- 5个按钮对应5种crash场景: Java Crash / Native Crash / Direct Java Core / Abort / OOM\\n\\n### 设计决策\\n- 所有crash操作在子线程执行，防止ANR和主线程污染\\n- `Coredump.getInstance().doCoredump(filename)` 使用时间戳+TID命名，利于调试多线程问题\\n- 实现 `Coredump.Listener` 异步接收core文件生成完成回调\\n\\n### 依赖关系\\n- 依赖 `penguin.opencore.sdk.Coredump` — Java层SDK单例\\n- 依赖 native library `native-lib` — 提供 `nativeCrashJNI` / `nativeAbortJNI`\\n- 通过Android布局文件 `R.layout.activity_main` 和 `R.id.button*` 绑定UI\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 2. `Coredump.java` — Java SDK Core API\\n\\n**文件路径:** `opencore/src/main/java/penguin/opencore/sdk/Coredump.java`\\n\\n### 类与函数\\n- **单例类:** `Coredump` — 通过 `private static final Coredump sInstance = new Coredump()` 实现\\n- **`init()`** — 初始化native层，创建两个 `HandlerThread`（`opencore-bg` 和 `opencore-post`），加载 `libopencore.so`\\n- **`enable(int type)` / `disable(int type)` / `isEnabled(int type)`** — 分别控制JAVA/NATIVE两种crash监控类型\\n- **`doCoredump(String filename)`** — 核心方法：发送 `CODE_COREDUMP` 事件到后台Handler，等待 `COREDUMPED` 条件\\n- **`doCompleted(String filename)`** — 发送 `CODE_COREDUMP_COMPLETED` 事件到post Handler，等待完成信号\\n- **`waitCore()` / `notifyCore()` / `waitCompleted()` / `notifyCompleted()`** — 基于 `Object mLock` 的 condition wait/notify 模式\\n- **`setListener(Listener)`** — 设置core文件生成完成后的Java回调\\n- **`setCoreDir/String flag/timeout/filter`** — 配置项setter（均通过native方法透传到底层）\\n- **`getCoreDir/flag/timeout/filter`** — 配置项getter\\n- **`coreFlagToString(int)` / `coreFilterToString(int)`** — 位标志的可读字符串化工具\\n- **内部类 `OpencoreHandler extends Handler`** — 处理 `CODE_COREDUMP`，调用 `nativeCoredump(tid, filename)`，然后 `notifyCore()`\\n- **内部类 `OpencorePostHandler extends Handler`** — 处理 `CODE_COREDUMP_COMPLETED`，调用 `onCompleted(filepath)`，然后 `notifyCompleted()`\\n- **内部类 `JavaCrashHandler implements Thread.UncaughtExceptionHandler`** — 捕获Java未处理异常，调用 `doCoredump()` 后 `Process.killProcess()` 自杀\\n\\n### 关键数据结构\\n- `mCondition` — 位标志：`STANDBY(0)`, `COREDUMPED(1<<0)`, `COREDUMP_COMPLETED(1<<1)`\\n- `mLock` — `Object` 作为monitor lock，用于wait/notify同步\\n- **FLAG常量:** `FLAG_CORE`, `FLAG_PROCESS_COMM`, `FLAG_PID`, `FLAG_THREAD_COMM`, `FLAG_TID`, `FLAG_TIMESTAMP`\\n- **FILTER常量:** `FILTER_SPECIAL_VMA`, `FILTER_FILE_VMA`, `FILTER_SHARED_VMA`, `FILTER_SANITIZER_SHADOW_VMA`, `FILTER_NON_READ_VMA`, `FILTER_SIGNAL_CONTEXT`, `FILTER_MINIDUMP`, `FILTER_JAVAHEAP_VMA`, `FILTER_JIT_CACHE_VMA`\\n- **`Listener` 接口:** `void onCompleted(String path)`\\n\\n### 设计决策\\n- **双HandlerThread架构：** 一个后台处理coredump核心逻辑（阻塞等待native完成），一个post处理回调通知（非阻塞）\\n- **同步等待模式：** `doCoredump()` 调用后block等待native层 `notifyCore()`，避免 caller 提前返回\\n- **JAVA crash handler替换：** 通过 `Thread.setDefaultUncaughtExceptionHandler()` 拦截所有Java异常\\n- **Singleton模式 + volatile ready flag：** `sReady` 保证init后多线程安全访问\\n- **静态native方法：** 所有native方法均为 `static native`，通过JNI全局注册\\n\\n### 依赖关系\\n- 完全依赖 JNI 调用 native C++ 层（`libopencore.so`）\\n- Android `Handler` / `HandlerThread` / `Looper` 体系实现异步消息队列\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 3. `opencore_jni.cpp` — JNI Bridge (Native Entry Point)\\n\\n**文件路径:** `opencore/src/main/cpp/opencore_jni.cpp`\\n\\n### 函数\\n- **`penguin_opencore_sdk_coredump_docallback(void* arg)`** — 在Java线程中执行回调：通过 `AndroidJNI::getJNIEnv()` 获取JNIEnv，构造 `jstring` 并调用 `CallStaticVoidMethod` 触发Java层 `callbackEvent(filepath)`\\n- **`penguin_opencore_sdk_coredump_callback(const char* filepath)`** — 创建 `opencore-cb` Java线程来执行docallback，使用 `pthread_join` 等待完成\\n- **`penguin_opencore_sdk_Coredump_nativeVersion()`** — 返回 `Opencore::GetVersion()`\\n- **`penguin_opencore_sdk_Coredump_nativeEnable/Disable()`** — 透传 `Opencore::Enable/Disable()`\\n- **`penguin_opencore_sdk_Coredump_nativeCoredump(jint tid, jstring filename)`** — 核心：调用 `Opencore::Dump(cstr, tid)`\\n- **`penguin_opencore_sdk_Coredump_nativeSetDir/Flag/Timeout/Filter()`** — setter透传\\n- **`penguin_opencore_sdk_Coredump_nativeGetDir/Flag/Timeout/Filter()`** — getter透传\\n- **`Java_penguin_opencore_sdk_Coredump_nativeInit(JNIEnv*, jclass)`** — JNI_OnLoad中注册native方法，注册全局回调 `gUser.gCallbackEvent`\\n- **`JNI_OnLoad(JavaVM*, void*)`** — 初始化 `AndroidJNI::init(vm)`，返回 `JNI_VERSION_1_4`\\n- **`opencore_ctor_init()`** — `__attribute__((constructor))`，inject模式自动启用\\n- **`opencore_dtor_init()`** — `__attribute__((destructor))`，inject模式自动禁用\\n\\n### 关键数据结构\\n- **`userdata` struct:** 持有 `gCoredump` (jclass全局引用) 和 `gCallbackEvent` (jmethodID)\\n- **`gUser`:** 全局单例，存储JNI反射查找结果\\n- **`gMethods[]`:** JNINativeMethod数组，15个native方法注册\\n\\n### 设计决策\\n- **全局回调架构：** `gUser` 保存全局J class引用和方法ID，避免重复查找\\n- **Java线程中回调：** 通过 `createJavaThread` 在JavaVM上下文中执行回调，解决native线程无法直接调用Java静态方法的问题\\n- **pthread_join同步：** `docallback` 在独立线程执行后join，确保回调完成再返回native层\\n- **Inject模式：** 通过 `INJECT_OPENCORE_DIR` 宏定义，支持 LD_PRELOAD 注入模式，自动配置并启用crash监控\\n- **Inject保存目录:** `/sdcard/Android/data/penguin.opencore.sdk/files`\\n\\n### 依赖关系\\n- 依赖 `opencore/opencore.h` — 核心C++类 `Opencore`\\n- 依赖 `eajnis/AndroidJNI.h` — Java线程创建工具\\n- 依赖 `eajnis/Log.h` — 日志宏\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 4. `AndroidJNI.cpp` — Android Java Thread Management\\n\\n**文件路径:** `opencore/src/main/cpp/eajni/android/AndroidJNI.cpp`\\n\\n### 类与函数\\n- **`AndroidJNI::getJNIEnv()`** — 获取当前线程的 `JNIEnv*`，通过 `JavaVM::GetEnv()` 绑定到 `JNI_VERSION_1_4`\\n- **`AndroidJNI::javaThreadShell(void* args)`** — 统一线程入口：Attach到JavaVM -> 调用 `entryFunction(userData)` -> Detach -> 释放name内存\\n- **`AndroidJNI::javaCreateThreadEtc(...)`** — 创建线程核心函数：分配3个void*参数数组，调用 `Thread::androidCreateRawThreadEtc`\\n- **`AndroidJNI::createJavaThread(const char* name, void(*start)(void*), void* arg)`** — 创建DETACHED线程\\n- **`AndroidJNI::createJavaThread(..., bool canwait)`** — 根据 `canwait` 决定 JOINABLE 或 DETACHED\\n- **`javaAttachThread(const char*, JNIEnv**)`** — Attach当前线程到JavaVM\\n- **`javaDetachThread(void)`** — Detach当前线程\\n\\n### 关键数据结构\\n- **`mJavaVM`:** static `JavaVM*`，跨线程共享\\n- **`android_thread_func_t`:** 函数指针类型 `void (*)(void*)`\\n- **`android_thread_id_t`:** `pthread_t` 的别名\\n\\n### 设计决策\\n- **JavaVM全局单例：** `mJavaVM` 在 `init(JavaVM*)` 时设置，所有线程共享\\n- **Attach/Detach配对：** 每个创建的Java线程必须配对调用Attach和Detach\\n- **线程参数打包：** 通过 `void** args` 数组打包 `(start_fn, userData, threadName)` 三个参数避免API膨胀\\n- **DETACHED vs JOINABLE：** Android线程默认DETACHED（不需要join回收），可wait模式支持join\\n- **基于Android Open Source Project(AOSP) 2007版本** — 遵循早期Android线程模型\\n\\n### 依赖关系\\n- 依赖 `pthread.h` — POSIX线程\\n- 依赖 AOSP `eajnis/Thread.h` — 底层原生线程创建\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 5. `opencore.h` — Core Dump Framework Header\\n\\n**文件路径:** `opencore/src/main/cpp/opencore/opencore.h`\\n\\n### 类与函数\\n- **`Opencore` class** — 核心抽象类\\n- **`Coredump(const char* filename)`** — 实例方法，触发core dump\\n- **`DoCoredump(const char* filename)`** — 虚函数，子类实现\\n- **`NeedFilterFile(VMA&)`** — 虚函数，判断VMA是否需要过滤\\n- **`getMachine()`** — 虚函数，返回ELF machine type\\n- **`IsFilterSegment(VMA&)`** — 基于通用filter标志判断VMA\\n- **`StopTheWorld(int pid)`** — 暂停进程所有线程（ptrace）\\n- **`StopTheThread(int tid)`** — 暂停单个线程\\n- **`Continue()`** — 恢复所有线程\\n- **`ParseMaps(int pid, vector<VMA>&)`** — 静态方法，解析 `/proc/<pid>/maps`\\n- **`HandleSignal(int, siginfo_t*, void*)`** — 信号处理静态入口\\n- **静态工具方法:** `GetInstance()`, `GetVersion()`, `Dump()` (重载4个版本), `Enable()`, `Disable()`, `IsEnabled()`, `IgnoreHandler()`, `SetDir/Callback/Flag/Timeout/Filter()`, `GetDir/Flag/Timeout/Filter()`, `TimeoutHandle()`\\n- **`RoundDown/ToundUp<T>()`** — 模板函数，对齐操作\\n\\n### 关键数据结构\\n- **`VirtualMemoryArea` struct:** `{ begin, end, flags[4], offset, major, minor, inode, file }` — 表示一块虚拟内存区域\\n- **`ThreadRecord` struct:** `{ pid, attached }` — 记录线程信息\\n- **`DumpOption` class:** `{ filename, pid, tid, siginfo, context }` — dump参数打包\\n- **`VMA_*` flags:** `VMA_NORMAL(0)`, `VMA_NULL(1<<0)`, `VMA_INCLUDE(1<<1)`\\n- **ELF Machine常量:** `EM_NONE(0)`, `EM_386(3)`, `EM_ARM(40)`, `EM_X86_64(62)`, `EM_AARCH64(183)`, `EM_RISCV(243)`\\n- **`GENMASK_UL(h,l)`** — 用于PAC mask计算：`((~0ULL) << (l)) & (~0ULL >> (64 - 1 - (h)))`\\n\\n### 设计决策\\n- **模板RoundUp/Down：** 使用模板加引用类型萃取实现通用对齐，无运行时开销\\n- **StopTheWorld暂停策略：** 使用ptrace暂停进程所有线程，确保dump时进程状态一致\\n- **\\\"only opencore-sdk append\\\"注释风格：** 标注SDK特有扩展（如ucontext_raw, siginfo, timeout等）\\n- **DumpOption参数打包：** 替代重载过多问题，便于扩展字段\\n- **FLAG/TIMEOUT/FILTER/Callback均可全局设置** — 通过static方法实现\\n\\n### 依赖关系\\n- 依赖标准库: `<inttypes.h>`, `<sys/types.h>`, `<stdint.h>`, `<unistd.h>`, `<signal.h>`, `<string>`, `<vector>`, `<type_traits>`\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 6. `arm64/opencore.cpp` — ARM64 Architecture Core Dump Implementation\\n\\n**文件路径:** `opencore/src/main/cpp/opencore/arm64/opencore.cpp`\\n\\n### 函数\\n- **`Opencore::CreateCorePrStatus(int pid)`** — 通过 `PTRACE_GETREGSET` 获取所有线程的通用寄存器\\n  - top thread优先使用 `ucontext` 中的regs（不通过ptrace）\\n  - 其他线程通过 `ptrace(PTRACE_GETREGSET, tid, NT_PRSTATUS, &ioVec)` 获取\\n  - 计算额外note大小: prstatus + siginfo + fpregset + tls + pac_mask + pac_enabled_keys + tagged_addr_ctrl\\n- **`Opencore::WriteCorePrStatus(FILE* fp)`** — 写入所有线程的NT_PRSTATUS note，每个线程写入: prstatus -> signal info -> fp regs -> TLS -> PAC -> MTE\\n- **`Opencore::IsSpecialFilterSegment(VMA& vma)`** — FILTER_MINIDUMP专用：检查PC和30个通用寄存器是否落在VMA范围内，如果是则VMA_INCLUDE\\n- **`Opencore::WriteCoreFpRegs(int tid, FILE* fp)`** — 通过 `PTRACE_GETREGSET(tid, NT_FPREGSET)` 获取浮点/向量寄存器\\n- **`Opencore::WriteCoreTLS(int tid, FILE* fp)`** — 通过 `PTRACE_GETREGSET(tid, NT_ARM_TLS)` 获取TLS寄存器\\n- **`Opencore::WriteCorePAC(int tid, FILE* fp)`** — 写入 `NT_ARM_PAC_MASK` 和 `NT_ARM_PAC_ENABLED_KEYS` notes\\n- **`Opencore::WriteCoreMTE(int tid, FILE* fp)`** — 写入 `NT_ARM_TAGGED_ADDR_CTRL` note（MTE地址控制）\\n- **`Opencore::Finish()`** — 清空prstatus，调用 `lp64::OpencoreImpl::Finish()`\\n\\n### 关键数据结构\\n- **`user_pac_mask` struct:** `{ data_mask, insn_mask }` — ARM64 PAC寄存器\\n- **`arm64::pt_regs`:** 寄存器结构体（通过ucontext获取）\\n- **NT_* constants:** `NT_FPREGSET(0x2)`, `NT_ARM_PAC_MASK(0x406)`, You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>/init-explore</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-06-07</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on durable technical signal:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)
- Concrete debugging or investigative findings from logs, traces, queue state, database rows, and code-path inspection

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored, discovered, confirmed, traced

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built or learned):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"
- "Observation queue for claude-mem session timed out waiting for an agent pool slot"
- "Fallback processing abandoned pending messages after Gemini and OpenRouter returned 404"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings with no follow-on finding
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- File related research that comes back empty or not found

If skipping, return an empty response only. Do not explain the skip in prose.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision | security_alert | security_note ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).

- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
